rationale: Gold's safe-haven channel is broken: Iran escalation transmits via oil-spike → inflation fear → hawkish Fed repricing rather than lifting bullion. September rate-hike odds remain live (~54%) and Warsh's first congressional testimony (July 14-15) alongside June CPI pose asymmetric hawkish event risk, with FOMC minutes already revealing several members pushing to hike. The NFP-driven relief bounce from $3,941 lows has stalled below broken moving averages at current $4,075 support; a failure here opens $3,942–$3,886.
key_drivers: June CPI binary event — upside surprise re-prices September hike odds
Oil-spike/Iran escalation acting as gold headwind via inflation channel
Broken technical structure — below 50d and 200d SMA, death cross risk
September Fed hike odds (~54%) still too elevated for sustained gold rally
NFP relief already priced — bounce stalling at resistance $4,108-4,127
